threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think it’s acceptable to call a kebab shop a 'doner house.' Haven't you seen the queue for the real ones around here?
Last night on the 35, saw a guy loudly arguing with the bus driver about how the seat cushions were 'plain evil'—never knew they could spark such passion!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I looked out my window and saw a spontaneous street concert with an accordionist, a painter, and a dog in a tutu.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a kebab place with a huge mural of David Bowie. It's like art meets late-night cravings.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they instinctively dodge the pigeon poop on 24th Street while still holding their burrito.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy serenading the 14 bus passengers with an accordion yesterday. Only in SF does a bus stop feel like a concert hall.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Most tourists think Gràcia is just a pretty place to snap pics, but they miss the real life happening in the corners, at the quiet plaça bars.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell a newbie by their struggle with the metro system; they still look at the map like it’s a treasure hunt.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still say 'Dalston Junction' instead of just 'the Junction' like the locals do. Trust me, you'll get used to it.
Last night on the 59, a guy serenaded everyone with old 90s R&B. Didn’t even care when the driver told him to stop. Best ride home in ages.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I walked past a street artist painting a mural while an old man danced flamenco next to a food stand. Pure Neukölln magic.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a late-night Döner place with a bouncer. It’s like a VIP club for kebabs—so Berlin.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know a local by their reaction to the Muni delays—smirks and eye rolls instead of shock. They expect it; it's part of the ride.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full tuxedo on the 22 today, chatting about the opera. Only in the Mission do you get a pre-show on your commute!

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think we all speak Catalan. Meanwhile, most locals switch to Spanish when they hear the English accent—it's like a secret code.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones staring at the metro map like it’s a treasure map, unaware that the signs are usually in both Catalan and Spanish.
